author: omerfirmak
content: if this goes thru,  `process_message_call` will need a touch up as well to align the top level call behaviour.

author: deme1744
content:
This seems to be the same issue we have been hitting in nimbus? see https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1498758081016889464/1501512775048888400

I saw there is a PR open to reverse the order: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2473

author: gurukamath
content:
This seems like a reasonable thing to expect imo. We don't access the delegation unless we absolutely have to. In this case, that means

1. There is enough gas including to pay for the delegation access
2. There is enough balance for the call to transfer value
3. The stack depth limit is not violated

author: m0_ox
content: EIP clarification and I linked here to the spec change and the test change: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11699

author: omerfirmak
content: do the tests cover top level calls failing due to insufficient funds too?

author: omerfirmak
content: seems like https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2473 only changes the inner calls' behaviour

author: draganrakita
content: wait wait, what is reasoning for changing order of this?

author: draganrakita
content: Delegation loading is done when gas for it is consumed

author: omerfirmak
content: not necessarily

author: omerfirmak
content: it only needs to be loaded when we know the bytecode will be executed

author: draganrakita
content: Every part of BAL works when gas is spend load the account, why make a difference here?

author: draganrakita
content: I know it can be different, but the reasoning is wrong imo

author: draganrakita
content: It needs to be loaded to check if it cold or warm, so you need to access the state to know about it

author: omerfirmak
content:
> It needs to be loaded to check if it cold or warm
why would you need to load the actual account state or bytecode from db to know if it was warm or cold?

author: omerfirmak
content: regardless of the warm/cold, if you don't have enough gas to access it, BAL won't include it.

author: omerfirmak
content: so, the node shouldn't need to load to figure out what the dynamic gas would be either

author: draganrakita
content: Bcs this is a place you load accounts, this is what all clients have implemented

author: draganrakita
content: <@126385519469789184> not sure why you want to change this, this is not small change and all clients would need to do it

author: omerfirmak
content: Sorry but they have implemented it in a suboptimal way then :/

author: draganrakita
content: No they didn't

author: draganrakita
content: It is implemented in right way, when you spend gas for loading it is included in BAL

author: draganrakita
content: This assumption is why it is in that place

author: omerfirmak
content: So, loading the bytecode regardless of actually needing it  or not is optimal?

author: draganrakita
content: You are breaking assumption here <@126385519469789184> this is what matters

author: omerfirmak
content: which is what exactly? If user pays for some state access, it should be in BAL?

author: draganrakita
content: Yes

author: omerfirmak
content: What is the benefit?

author: draganrakita
content: And btw we are talking about edge case here, this is not usual path you are talking about

author: draganrakita
content:
> What is the benefit?
Not breaking assumptions

author: omerfirmak
content: I am not saying that this is a huge deal, just the natural thing to expect

author: draganrakita
content: Lets a least discuss this with broader crowd, this is not small change as all clients have checks in different places

author: draganrakita
content:
>  am not saying that this is a huge deal, just the natural thing to expect
I would say expectations are different here.

author: omerfirmak
content: of course, I just brought it up.

author: omerfirmak
content: Looks like it. I think of BALs as the smallest set of state interactions that nodes can to do in order to execute a block.

author: omerfirmak
content:
> Not breaking assumptions

if you actually think of it as "checking that the caller has the funds to cover the transfer before doing anything else ~~(incl target related gas checks~~)", it doesn't really break any assumptions.

author: omerfirmak
content: ~~actually with that thinking even the target account should be omitted from BAL when that check fails~~

author: m0_ox
content: Exceptional halt / instruction validity is done before any insufficient fund checks. The target is loaded to check its code, etc. There is a boundary for this earlier in the static checks but if you clear enough gas for that you check if account is alive, etc, so no the target would not be omitted if this check fails.

author: m0_ox
content: There are a few cases where if we check balance we can make things more optimized, but for the sake of simplicity and not breaking previous assumptions, the gain is not worth the increase in complexity. I think this suggestion inches into this territory so while it is an optimization, it is worth discussing whether this is worth the added complexity across client teams.

author: omerfirmak
content:
> Exceptional halt / instruction validity is done before any insufficient fund checks
that is just one way of doing it tho. it doesn't have to be that way. an implementation might very well just do the fund check first and avoid doing any target related state lookups and it wouldn't have created a consensus issue before BALs.

author: omerfirmak
content: wait, insufficient funds don't consume all forwarded gas. ignore that:)

author: m0_ox
content: lol just erased my response 👍🏽

author: omerfirmak
content: my bad:)

author: m0_ox
content: all good

author: m0_ox
content: But yeah these boundaries were discussed back in the BAL breakouts as well. There are some advantages perhaps but at most of these points we discussed that the least complexity makes sense. I think this 7702 case is an interesting one. I can see both sides of the argument. I'd like to hear the push back before we enshrine something like this though.
